calendar.matters:
PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE CALENDAR FOR ATTORNEY JOHN DAVID COLLINS
Off Calendar May-27-2022 Inactivate Attorney

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JOHN DAVID COLLINS
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Continued To May-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-07-2022 Vacated

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Jun-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GUARDIAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's MOTION TO TRANSFER:

Defendant Guardian's motion to sever the claim for writ of mandate against the Commissioner of the Department of Insurance and transfer the remainder of the case (breach of contract and bad faith causes of action) against defendant Guardian to Santa Clara County is granted. (See David v. Medtronic, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 734, 741 [severing claims against physician for indirectly endorsing product from claims against manufacturer defendant for products liability].)

The Court grants judicial notice of exhibits 1 through 3 per Evidence Code 452(d)(1) [judicial notice of "[r]ecords of [] any court of this state."] The Court agrees with plaintiff that these prior rulings are not binding precedent and the citation to exhibit 2 could be construed as a violation of Rule of Court 8.1115(a). In any event, the instant ruling is based on the facts of this case and not on those prior rulings.

There is no basis for mandatory joinder of the writ of mandate claim against the Commissioner and the breach of contract and bad faith claims against the insurer. Compulsory joinder is required where "if (1) in his absence complete relief cannot be accorded among those already parties" (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 389(a).) In this case, joinder of the Commissioner is unnecessary to accord complete relief. The claims against the Commissioner and insurer are transactionally and temporally distinct. The Commissioner approved the terms of the policy in December 1998, more than two decades before plaintiff's alleged bad faith claims arose in 2020. The Commissioner is not an indispensable party because the Court will construe the terms of plaintiff's policy to conform with California law even if the Commissioner approved non-conforming language. (See Ins. Code sec. 10390; Interinsurance Exchange of the Automobile Club of So. Cal. v. Ohio Casualty Ins. Co. (1962) 58 Cal.2d 142, 148 [holding statutory and decisional law "are read into each policy" and "become a part of the contract with full binding effect upon each party."]. Plaintiff speculates that the Commissioner is a necessary party to monitor defendant's future compliance with the law. If plaintiff prevails, a Superior Court can insure that plaintiff's rights are vindicated. (See Code Civ. Proc. secs. 128(a)(8); 187.)

Plaintiff also fails to meet the permissive joinder requirements. The writ and contract claims do not arise out of the "same transaction, occurrence, or series of transactions or occurrences." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 379(a).) The federal cases upon which Plaintiff relies do not support his position, as they held only that the claims were not fraudulently joined because the plaintiff stated a cause of action against both defendants; in any event, they are not binding on this Court. The Court further notes that keeping these claims together does not further judicial economy. The writ claim will be adjudicated by motion or court trial while the bad faith claim will be resolved by jury trial. There will necessarily be two proceedings. Since everything pertaining to the bad faith claims occurred in Santa Clara County, those claims should be resolved there. The Court exercises its discretion under Code of Civil Procedure section 379.5 and severs those claims and transfers them to that court.
The Clerk of the Court is ordered to assign a new case number to the remaining claim for writ of mandate against the Commissioner.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GUARDIAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The verified application of John David Collins for admission as counsel pro hac vice for defendant is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
